hard_subset_performance.interpretation: Cross-pair absolute frame-logit discrimination fails on the Stage176 transition cohort.

within_pair_performance.comparison: compatible frame_logit > incompatible frame_logit within the same pair_id
within_pair_performance.interpretation: Strong pair-relative ordering is preserved despite weak hard-cohort absolute comparability.

established_findings: The frame head has strong global clean-dev discrimination.
Same-pair compatible-versus-incompatible ranking is approximately 94 percent.
Absolute cross-pair discrimination fails on the hard-39 cohort.
The evidence does not support global frame-output collapse.
The actionable defect is inter-pair absolute score comparability, not absence of pair-relative distinction.
Neither a fixed threshold nor post-hoc calibration is supported as a separator of the Stage176 beneficial and harmful transitions.
A frame-head-specific same-pair relative objective is eligible for feasibility audit.
The closed Stage174-C final-classifier SUPPORT-logit pairwise path must not be reopened.
interpretation: Stage177-A is an observational, single-seed internal clean-dev audit.
Pair-relative ordering and cross-pair absolute discrimination measure different properties; the former is strong while the latter is weak on the hard cohort.
The result authorizes only a clean frame-logit pairwise-objective feasibility audit, not implementation or training.

next_stage.purpose: Audit clean deterministic same-pair frame-logit supervision topology, baseline gaps, and default-off implementation feasibility.
